<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>il risultato ottenuto è eccellente. posso d'ora in poi dirti semplicemente /confronto "mese 25 - mese 26" per avere un html corrispondente?</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-03-23</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
Report creato e aperto: `/chuck/Genova/febbraio-2025-vs-2026.html`

---

**Cosa contiene il report:**

**5 delta card in cima** — snapshot immediato dei KPI con frecce su/giù

**Barre comparative visive** — ricavi, costi, MOL, spedizioni 2025 vs 2026 a confronto visivo

**Tabella servizi feb 2025** (da sped.xlsx — dati certi):

| | N sped | Ricavi | MOL% |
|--|--|--|--|
| Export Aereo | 181 | €212k | 23,2% |
| Export Mare | 56 | €108k | **27,9%** |
| Crosstrade Aereo | 21 | €14k | 16,5% |
| Crosstrade Mare | 7 | €14k | 11,7% |

**Mix aereo/marittimo feb 2026** (da BI): aereo 77,5% ricavi ma solo 73,4% MOL → **il marittimo è più efficiente per margine**

**3 insight box** — volume record, marginalità in calo, analisi mix

**Lettura sintetica:** i ricavi quasi raddoppiano (+98,6%), ma i costi crescono più velocemente (+104,3%) → MOL% scende da 23,0% a 20,8%. La crescita c'è, ma costa. Da guardare: quali clienti/servizi stanno portando il volume in più ma a margine inferiore.

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

content.thinking:
The user is asking for a progress summary. Looking at the context:

1. The user asked if they could use "/confronto 'mese 25 - mese 26'" as a shorthand command
2. Claude responded that a report was created and opened at `/chuck/Genova/febbraio-2025-vs-2026.html`
3. The response details what the report contains:
   - 5 delta cards showing KPIs with up/down arrows
   - Visual comparative bars for revenue, costs, MOL, shipments 2025 vs 2026
   - A services table for Feb 2025 from sped.xlsx with actual data
   - Air/maritime mix for Feb 2026 from BI data
   - 3 insight boxes covering volume records, margin decline, and mix analysis
   - Synthetic reading showing revenues nearly doubled (+98.6%) but costs grew faster (+104.3%), leading to MOL% declining from 23.0% to 20.8%

So the work that was done:
- Created an HTML comparison report for February 2025 vs 2026
- Integrated data from sped.xlsx (2025 actual data) and BI system (2026 data)
- Built visualizations and analysis components
- Established a reusable workflow/command for generating these reports

What was learned:
- How to structure month-over-month comparison reports
- Data sources: sped.xlsx for historical data, BI for current year data
- Key metrics: shipments, revenues, costs, MOL%, service breakdown, air/maritime mix
- The business insight: growth is strong but margin efficiency is declining

What's next:
- The user confirmed they want to use this as a repeatable pattern going forward
- Future comparisons can be generated with the /confronto command

Let me write this as a progress summary from the observer's perspective.

content.text:
```xml
<summary>
  <request>Generate HTML comparison report for February 2025 vs 2026 and establish reusable /confronto workflow</request>
  <investigated>Multi-source data integration for comparative analysis spanning sped.xlsx (historical shipment data) and BI system (current year data) for Genova operations. Examined revenue trends, cost structures, MOL percentages, service breakdowns (Export/Crosstrade, Aereo/Mare), and shipment volumes across the two February periods.</investigated>
  <learned>The comparison report architecture combines five key elements: delta KPI cards with directional indicators, visual comparative bar charts, detailed service-level tables with shipment counts and margins, air/maritime mix analysis showing efficiency differences (maritime yields higher MOL% despite lower revenue share), and synthesized insight boxes. Data reveals revenues grew 98.6% year-over-year but costs outpaced at 104.3%, compressing MOL% from 23.0% to 20.8%. Export Mare service shows strongest margin performance at 27.9% MOL.</learned>
  <completed>Created and deployed `/chuck/Genova/febbraio-2025-vs-2026.html` with full comparative analysis including 5 delta cards, visual bar comparisons, February 2025 service table (181 Export Aereo shipments at €212k, 56 Export Mare at €108k, plus Crosstrade segments), February 2026 mix breakdown showing 77.5% aereo revenue concentration, and 3 insight boxes highlighting volume growth, margin compression, and mix implications. Established /confronto command pattern for future month-to-month comparisons with user-confirmed excellent output quality.</completed>
  <next_steps>The /confronto workflow is now operational and ready for ad-hoc use. User can invoke future comparisons by specifying month pairs (e.g., "mese 25 - mese 26") to generate equivalent HTML reports. The pattern is established and validated for ongoing comparative analysis needs.</next_steps>
  <notes>The report reveals a critical business insight: while volume growth is strong, the margin efficiency is declining as costs scale faster than revenues. The air/maritime mix analysis suggests optimization opportunities—maritime operations deliver better MOL% (27.9% for Export Mare vs 23.2% for Export Aereo) but represent smaller revenue share. This points to potential strategic questions about which clients or services are driving high-volume, low-margin growth.</notes>
</summary>
```

  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/confronto/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"# Skill: /confronto\\n\\n## Quando usare questa skill\\nQuando Roy scrive `/confronto \\\"mese anno - mese anno\\\"` o varianti simili.\\nEsempi validi:\\n- `/confronto \\\"febbraio 2025 - febbraio 2026\\\"`\\n- `/confronto \\\"feb 25 - feb 26\\\"`\\n- `/confronto \\\"marzo 2025 - marzo 2026\\\"`\\n\\n## Parametri\\nFormato: `[mese1] [anno1] - [mese2] [anno2]`\\n- Accetta nomi completi (febbraio) o abbreviazioni (feb)\\n- Accetta anni a 2 o 4 cifre (25 → 2025, 26 → 2026)\\n- Se mancano, chiedi: \\\"Quale confronto vuoi? Es: febbraio 2025 - febbraio 2026\\\"\\n\\n## Mappatura mesi\\n| Nome | Abbreviazione BI | N° mese |\\n|------|-----------------|---------|\\n| gennaio | gen | 1 |\\n| febbraio | feb | 2 |\\n| marzo | mar | 3 |\\n| aprile | apr | 4 |\\n| maggio | mag | 5 |\\n| giugno | giu | 6 |\\n| luglio | lug | 7 |\\n| agosto | ago | 8 |\\n| settembre | set | 9 |\\n| ottobre | ott | 10 |\\n| novembre | nov | 11 |\\n| dicembre | dic | 12 |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Procedura Completa\\n\\n### Step 1 — Estrai dati dal periodo \\\"vecchio\\\" (sped.xlsx, se è 2025)\\n\\nSe il primo periodo è 2025, usa Python su `/Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/sped.xlsx`:\\n\\n```python\\nimport pandas as pd\\ndf = pd.read_excel('/Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/sped.xlsx', sheet_name=0)\\ndf['Competenza'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Competenza'], errors='coerce')\\nmask = (df['Competenza'].dt.year == ANNO) & (df['Competenza'].dt.month == MESE_NUM)\\nperiodo = df[mask]\\n\\nrcol = 'Ricavi Sped e Raggruppate (DP)'\\nccol = 'Costi Sped e Raggruppate (DP)'\\nmcol = 'Saldo Totale Sped'\\n\\n# Totali\\nricavi = periodo[rcol].sum()\\ncosti = periodo[ccol].sum()\\nmol = periodo[mcol].sum()\\nmol_pct = mol / ricavi * 100\\nn_sped = len(periodo)\\n\\n# Per servizio\\nsvc = periodo.groupby('Servizio').agg(\\n    N=('Spedizione', 'count'),\\n    Ricavi=(rcol, 'sum'),\\n    Costi=(ccol, 'sum'),\\n    MOL=(mcol, 'sum')\\n).copy()\\nsvc['MOL%'] = svc['MOL'] / svc['Ricavi'] * 100\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2 — Estrai dati dal BI per confronto YoY\\n\\nNaviga al BI Qlik Sense e seleziona il mese del secondo periodo (o entrambi se necessario).\\nSegui la procedura della skill `dati-bi` per login e navigazione.\\n\\n**URL Dashboard**: `https://beoneanalytics.novasystems.it/sense/app/142567b7-4344-4e56-8943-7f5710260b65/sheet/2d53e597-a111-40bb-8622-3654469c8bc0/state/analysis`\\n\\nDopo aver selezionato il mese, leggi dal snapshot:\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Ricavi 2026 (DP) [VAL_2026]` e `[VAL_2025] Ricavi 2025`\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Costi 2026 (DP) [VAL_2026]` e `[VAL_2025] Costi 2025`\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Saldo 2026 (DP) [VAL_2026]` e `[VAL_2025] Saldo 2025`\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Saldo % 2026 [VAL%_2026]` e `[VAL%_2025] Saldo % 2025`\\n- `Grafico a barre Traffico [N_2025] [N_2026]`\\n- `Grafico a torta Incidenza Ricavi Aereo [X]% Marittimo [Y]%`\\n- `Grafico a torta Incidenza Saldo Aereo [X]% Marittimo [Y]%`\\n\\n**Nota**: il BI mostra sempre 2026 vs 2025. Se il confronto richiesto è diverso (es. 2024 vs 2025), adatta la logica.\\n\\n### Step 3 — Calcola delta\\n\\n```\\ndelta_ricavi = ricavi_2 - ricavi_1\\ndelta_ricavi_pct = (ricavi_2 / ricavi_1 - 1) * 100\\ndelta_mol = mol_2 - mol_1\\ndelta_mol_pct = (mol_2 / mol_1 - 1) * 100\\ndelta_molp = molp_2 - molp_1  # in pp\\ndelta_sped = sped_2 - sped_1\\ndelta_sped_pct = (sped_2 / sped_1 - 1) * 100\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4 — Genera HTML e salva\\n\\nSalva in: `/Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/confronto-[mese]-[anno1]-vs-[anno2].html`\\n\\nIl report HTML deve includere:\\n1. **Header** con titolo e badge \\\"Confronto YoY\\\"\\n2. **5 delta card** in cima: Spedizioni, Ricavi, Costi, MOL€, MOL%\\n   - Colore verde (▲) se miglioramento, rosso (▼) se peggioramento\\n   - MOL€ su = verde; MOL% su = verde; costi su = rosso (trend negativo)\\n3. **Barre comparative visive** — Ricavi, Costi, MOL, Spedizioni (periodo1 vs periodo2)\\n4. **Tabella servizi** del periodo 1 (se dati da sped.xlsx disponibili)\\n5. **Mix Aereo/Marittimo** del periodo più recente (da BI)\\n6. **3 insight box** — volume, marginalità, mix\\n7. **Tabella riepilogo** con tutti i KPI, delta e trend\\n8. **Footer** con fonte dati e data elaborazione\\n\\n### Stile HTML (dark theme — usare questo esatto CSS)\\n```css\\n:root {\\n  --bg: #0d1117; --surface: #161b22; --border: #30363d;\\n  --accent: #58a6ff; --green: #3fb950; --red: #f85149;\\n  --yellow: #e3b341; --text: #e6edf3; --muted: #8b949e;\\n  --card-bg: #1c2128;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nUsa lo stesso stile del file esistente:\\n`/Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/febbraio-2025-vs-2026.html`\\ncome riferimento per struttura e CSS completo.\\n\\n### Step 5 — Apri il file\\n```bash\\nopen /Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/confronto-[mese]-[anno1]-vs-[anno2].html\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Note Tecniche\\n- sped.xlsx contiene SOLO dati 2025 (colonna `Competenza`)\\n- Per confronti che coinvolgono solo 2026 o anni futuri, usa esclusivamente il BI\\n- Il BI mostra KPI 2026 (DP) vs 2025 come riferimento fisso — sfrutta questa struttura\\n- I valori di MOL% nel BI sono già in percentuale (es. 20,8%)\\n- I valori MOL% in sped.xlsx sono in decimale (0.208) → moltiplica × 100\\n- Se la sessione Playwright è già attiva, salta il login e naviga direttamente\\n- Valore medio per spedizione = Ricavi totali / N spedizioni (utile per il report)\\n\\n## Output Atteso\\nFile HTML salvato + messaggio a Roy con:\\n- Percorso file\\n- 3-4 takeaway chiave (bullet point)\\n- Eventuale alert se MOL% < 20% o costi crescono > ricavi\\n\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"create\",\"filePath\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/confronto/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"# Skill: /confronto\\n\\n## Quando usare questa skill\\nQuando Roy scrive `/confronto \\\"mese anno - mese anno\\\"` o varianti simili.\\nEsempi validi:\\n- `/confronto \\\"febbraio 2025 - febbraio 2026\\\"`\\n- `/confronto \\\"feb 25 - feb 26\\\"`\\n- `/confronto \\\"marzo 2025 - marzo 2026\\\"`\\n\\n## Parametri\\nFormato: `[mese1] [anno1] - [mese2] [anno2]`\\n- Accetta nomi completi (febbraio) o abbreviazioni (feb)\\n- Accetta anni a 2 o 4 cifre (25 → 2025, 26 → 2026)\\n- Se mancano, chiedi: \\\"Quale confronto vuoi? (DP)'\\nccol = 'Costi Sped e Raggruppate (DP)'\\nmcol = 'Saldo Totale Sped'\\n\\n# Totali\\nricavi = periodo[rcol].sum()\\ncosti = periodo[ccol].sum()\\nmol = periodo[mcol].sum()\\nmol_pct = mol / ricavi * 100\\nn_sped = len(periodo)\\n\\n# Per servizio\\nsvc = periodo.groupby('Servizio').agg(\\n    N=('Spedizione', 'count'),\\n    Ricavi=(rcol, 'sum'),\\n    Costi=(ccol, 'sum'),\\n    MOL=(mcol, 'sum')\\n).copy()\\nsvc['MOL%'] = svc['MOL'] / svc['Ricavi'] * 100\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2 — Estrai dati dal BI per confronto YoY\\n\\nNaviga al BI Qlik Sense e seleziona il mese del secondo periodo (o entrambi se necessario).\\nSegui la procedura della skill `dati-bi` per login e navigazione.\\n\\n**URL Dashboard**: `https://beoneanalytics.novasystems.it/sense/app/142567b7-4344-4e56-8943-7f5710260b65/sheet/2d53e597-a111-40bb-8622-3654469c8bc0/state/analysis`\\n\\nDopo aver selezionato il mese, leggi dal snapshot:\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Ricavi 2026 (DP) [VAL_2026]` e `[VAL_2025] Ricavi 2025`\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Costi 2026 (DP) [VAL_2026]` e `[VAL_2025] Costi 2025`\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Saldo 2026 (DP) [VAL_2026]` e `[VAL_2025] Saldo 2025`\\n- `KPI Nessun titolo Saldo % 2026 [VAL%_2026]` e `[VAL%_2025] Saldo % 2025`\\n- `Grafico a barre Traffico [N_2025] [N_2026]`\\n- `Grafico a torta Incidenza Ricavi Aereo [X]% Marittimo [Y]%`\\n- `Grafico a torta Incidenza Saldo Aereo [X]% Marittimo [Y]%`\\n\\n**Nota**: il BI mostra sempre 2026 vs 2025. Se il confronto richiesto è diverso (es. 2024 vs 2025), adatta la logica.\\n\\n### Step 3 — Calcola delta\\n\\n```\\ndelta_ricavi = ricavi_2 - ricavi_1\\ndelta_ricavi_pct = (ricavi_2 / ricavi_1 - 1) * 100\\ndelta_mol = mol_2 - mol_1\\ndelta_mol_pct = (mol_2 / mol_1 - 1) * 100\\ndelta_molp = molp_2 - molp_1  # in pp\\ndelta_sped = sped_2 - sped_1\\ndelta_sped_pct = (sped_2 / sped_1 - 1) * 100\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4 — Genera HTML e salva\\n\\nSalva in: `/Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/confronto-[mese]-[anno1]-vs-[anno2].html`\\n\\nIl report HTML deve includere:\\n1. **Header** con titolo e badge \\\"Confronto YoY\\\"\\n2. **5 delta card** in cima: Spedizioni, Ricavi, Costi, MOL€, MOL%\\n   - Colore verde (▲) se miglioramento, rosso (▼) se peggioramento\\n   - MOL€ su = verde; MOL% su = verde; costi su = rosso (trend negativo)\\n3. **Barre comparative visive** — Ricavi, Costi, MOL, Spedizioni (periodo1 vs periodo2)\\n4. **Tabella servizi** del periodo 1 (se dati da sped.xlsx disponibili)\\n5. **Mix Aereo/Marittimo** del periodo più recente (da BI)\\n6. **3 insight box** — volume, marginalità, mix\\n7. **Tabella riepilogo** con tutti i KPI, delta e trend\\n8. **Footer** con fonte dati e data elaborazione\\n\\n### Stile HTML (dark theme — usare questo esatto CSS)\\n```css\\n:root {\\n  --bg: #0d1117; --surface: #161b22; --border: #30363d;\\n  --accent: #58a6ff; --green: #3fb950; --red: #f85149;\\n  --yellow: #e3b341; --text: #e6edf3; --muted: #8b949e;\\n  --card-bg: #1c2128;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nUsa lo stesso stile del file esistente:\\n`/Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/febbraio-2025-vs-2026.html`\\ncome riferimento per struttura e CSS completo.\\n\\n### Step 5 — Apri il file\\n```bash\\nopen /Users/royrigamonti/chuck/Genova/confronto-[mese]-[anno1]-vs-[anno2].html\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Note Tecniche\\n- sped.xlsx contiene SOLO dati 2025 (colonna `Competenza`)\\n- Per confronti che coinvolgono solo 2026 o anni futuri, usa esclusivamente il BI\\n- Il BI mostra KPI 2026 (DP) vs 2025 come riferimento fisso — sfrutta questa struttura\\n- I valori di MOL% nel BI sono già in percentuale (es. 20,8%)\\n- I valori MOL% in sped.xlsx sono in decimale (0.208) → moltiplica × 100\\n- Se la sessione Playwright è già attiva, salta il login e naviga direttamente\\n- Valore medio per spedizione = Ricavi totali / N spedizioni (utile per il report)\\n\\n## Output Atteso\\nFile HTML salvato + messaggio a Roy con:\\n- Percorso file\\n- 3-4 takeaway chiave (bullet point)\\n- Eventuale alert se MOL% < 20% o costi crescono > ricavi\\n\"}"</parameters>
